questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the point $M(h, k)$ lie on the line $2 x+3 y=5$ such that $|M A-M B|$ is maximum where $A(2,3)$ and $B(1,2)$, then find the value of $(3 h+2 k)$.
questions.solution: As, $\quad|M A-M B| \leq A B$ $\therefore \quad|M A-M B|_{\text {maximum }}=A B$, which is possible when $M$ is the point of intersection of line joining $A(1,2), B(2,3)$ and $2 x+3 y=5$ So, $M\left(x=\frac{2}{5}, y=\frac{7}{5}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A line with positive rational slope, which passes through the point $(6,0)$ and at a distance of 5 units from the point $(1,3)$ is equal to $\frac{p}{q}$ (where $p, q$ are co-prime), then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{rlrl}y & =m(x-6) \\ m x-y-6 m & =0 \Rightarrow\left|\frac{5 m+3}{\sqrt{1+m^2}}\right|=5 \\ (5 m+3)^2 & =25+25 m^2 \Rightarrow 9+30 m=25 \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{p}{q} & \equiv \frac{8}{15} \\ \therefore & p+q & =23\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find number of integral values of $\lambda$ if $(\lambda, \lambda+1)$ is an interior points of $\triangle A B C$, where $A \equiv(0,3), B \equiv(-2,0)$ and $C \equiv(6,1)$.
questions.solution:
Since $(\lambda, \lambda+1)$ lies on $y=x+1$ equation of $A B: 3 x-2 y+6=0 ; B C: x-8 y+2=0 ; A C: x+3 y-9=0$
Line $y=x+1$ cuts $A C$ at $P\left(\frac{3}{2}, \frac{5}{2}\right)$ cut $B C$ at $Q\left(\frac{-6}{7}, \frac{1}{7}\right)$. Hence $\lambda \in\left(\frac{-6}{7}, \frac{3}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$A(3,4), B(0,0)$ and $C(3,0)$ are vertices of $\triangle A B C$. If ' $P$ ' is the point inside the $\triangle A B C$, such that $d(P, B C) \leq m i n$. $\{d(P, A B), d(P, A C)\}$. Then the maximum of $d(P, B C)$ is.
(where $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})$ represent distance between P and BC ).
questions.solution:
Here $B P$ and $C P$ are angular bisectors. Maximum of $d(P, B C)$ occurs, when $P$ is incentre of $\triangle A B C$.
$\therefore \quad$ Maximum of $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})=\mathrm{PN}=$ ordinate of incentre $=1$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A light beam emanating from the point $A(3,10)$ reflects from the straight line $2 x+y-6=0$ and then passes through the point $B(4,3)$. The equation of the reflected beam is $x+3 y-\lambda=0$, then find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution:
Image of $A(3,10)$ in $2 x+y-6=0$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x-3}{2}=\frac{y-10}{1}=-2\left(\frac{6+10-6}{2^2+1^2}\right) \\ & \frac{x-3}{2}=\frac{y-10}{1}=-4 \\ & A^{\prime}=(-5,6)\end{aligned}$ Equation of $A^{\prime} B$ is $y-3=\left(\frac{6-3}{-5-4}\right)(x-4)$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow y-3=-\frac{1}{3}(x-4) \\ & 3 y-9=-x+4 \\ & \Rightarrow x+3 y-13=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\mathrm{A}(\alpha, 0), \mathrm{B}(\beta, 0), \mathrm{C}(\gamma, 0), \mathrm{D}(\delta, 0)$ and $\alpha, \beta$ are the roots of equation $a x^2+2 \mathrm{hx}+\mathrm{b}=0$ while $\gamma, \delta$ are the those of $a_1 x^2+2 h_1 x+b_1=0$ if $C$ and $D$ divides $A B$ in the ratio $\lambda: 1$ and $\mu: 1$ respectively also $a b_1$, $h h_1, a_1 b$ are in A.P., then $\lambda+\mu$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \alpha+\beta=-\frac{2 h}{a} \\ & \alpha \beta=\frac{b}{a} \\ & \gamma+\delta=-\frac{2 h_1}{a_1} \\ & \gamma \delta=\frac{b_1}{a_1} \\ & \text { given } \gamma=\frac{\lambda \beta+\alpha}{\lambda+1} \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{\gamma-\alpha}{\beta-\gamma} \\ & \text { similarly } \mu=\frac{\delta-\alpha}{\beta-\delta} \\ & \text { Now } \lambda+\mu=\frac{(\gamma-\alpha)(\beta-\delta)+(\beta-\gamma)(\delta-\alpha)}{(\beta-\gamma)(\beta-\delta)} \\ & =\frac{2}{a a_1}\left(2 h_1-a_1 b-a b_1\right) \\ & (\beta-\gamma)(\beta-\delta)\end{aligned}=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: On the straight line $y=x+2$, a point $(a, b)$ is such that the sum of the square of distances from the straight lines $3 x-4 y+8=0$ and $3 x-y-1=0$ is least, then find value of $11(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
Point be $(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})$ but it lies on $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}+2$ So, $(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{x}+2)$
$F(x)=\left[\frac{3 x-4(x+2)+8}{\sqrt{3^2+4^2}}\right]^2+\left[\frac{3 x-(x+2)-1}{\sqrt{3^2+1^2}}\right]^2=\frac{2 x^2+5\left[4 x^2-12 x+9\right]}{50}=\frac{22\left[\left(x-\frac{30}{22}\right)^2-\frac{900}{484}\right]+45}{50}$
$F(x)$ is minimum at $x=\frac{15}{11}$. So point is $\left(\frac{15}{11}, \frac{37}{11}\right)=(a, b)$
$11(a+b)=52$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the straight lines joining the origin and the points of intersection of the curve $5 x^2+12 x y-6 y^2+4 x-2 y+3=0$ and $x+k y-1=0$ are equally inclined to the $x$-axis, then find the value of $|\mathrm{k}|$.
questions.solution:
Homogenize $5 x^2+12 x y-6 y^2+4 x-2 y+3=0$ by $x+k y=1$
$5 x^2+12 x y-6 y^2+4 x(x+k y)-2 y(x+k y)+3(x+k y)^2=0$
it is equally indined with $x$-axes hence coeff. $x y=0 \Rightarrow 12+4 k-2+6 k=0 \Rightarrow k=-1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I_1$ be the line $4 x+3 y=3$ and $I_2$ be the line $y=8 x$. $L_1$ is the line formed by reflecting $I_1$ across the line $y=x$ and $L_2$ is the line formed by reflecting $I_2$ across the $x$-axis. If $\theta$ is the acute angle between $L_1$ and $L_2$ such that $\tan \theta=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{b}}$, where a and b are coprime then find $(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$.
questions.solution:
$I_1: 4 x+3 y=3$
$f(x)=y=\frac{3-4 x}{3}....(1)$
since $f(x)$ and $f^{-1}(x)$ are the mirror images of each other in the line $y=x$ hence we find $f^{-1}(x)$.
$\text { now } y=f(x) \Rightarrow f^{-1}(y)=x$
from (1) $x=\frac{3(1-y)}{4} ; f^{-1}(y)=\frac{3(1-y)}{4}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore f^{-1}(x)=\frac{3(1-x)}{4} \\
& 4 y=3-3 x L_1=3 x+4 y-3=0, m_1=-3 / 4
\end{aligned}$
|||ly $L_2=y=-8 x$ with $m_2=-8$
if $\theta$ is the acute angle between the lines
$\tan \theta=\left|\frac{\mathrm{m}_2-\mathrm{m}_1}{1+\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{~m}_2}\right|=\left|\frac{-8+\frac{3}{4}}{1+(-8)\left(-\frac{3}{4}\right)}\right|=\left|\frac{-29}{28}\right| \Rightarrow \frac{29}{28}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow  a=29 \text { and } b=28 \\ & \therefore a+b=29+28=57\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The extremities of a diagonal of a rectangle are $(0,0)$ and $(4,4)$. The locus of the extremities of the other diagonal is $x^2+y^2-\lambda_1 x-\lambda_2 y=0$, then find the value of $\left(\lambda_1+\lambda_2\right)$.
questions.solution: The locus of the extremities of the other diagonal is the eirele with given diagonal as diameter. $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow (x-0)(x-4)+(y-4)(y-0) =0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y =0 \\ & \text {Aliter: } (A E)^2 =(D E)^2 \\ & (h-2)^2+(k-2)^2 =(2 \sqrt{2})^2 \\ & x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y =0 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let points $A$ and $C$ be lying on straight lines $4 y=3 x$ and $x=0$ respectively. If point $(6,2)$ is lying on the straight line $A C$ and rhombus $O A B C$ is completed where $O$ is the origin and $B$ lies in first quadrant, then find the area of the rhombus (in sq. units).
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} 2 \theta & =\frac{\pi}{2}-\alpha \\ \tan 2 \theta & =\cot \alpha=\frac{4}{3} \\ \frac{2 \tan \theta}{1-\tan ^2 \theta} & =\frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow \tan \theta=\frac{1}{2} \end{aligned} $$ Now, slope of $O B=\tan (\theta+\alpha)=\frac{\tan \theta+\tan \alpha}{1-\tan \theta \tan \alpha}$ $$ =\frac{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{3}{4}}{1-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)}=2 $$ $\therefore$ Slope of $A C=\frac{-1}{2}$ Equation of $A C \Rightarrow y-2=-\frac{1}{2}(x-6)$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad x+2 y=10 $$ $\begin{gathered}C \equiv(0,5) \\ \text { Area of rhombus }=2 \times \operatorname{Ar} .(\triangle O A C)=\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 5 \times \sin 2 \theta=25 \times \frac{4}{5}=20\end{gathered}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A straight line passing through origin $O$ meets the lines $4 x+2 y=3$ and $2 x+y+6=0$ at the points $P$ and $Q$ respectively, then the point $O$ divides the segment $P Q$ in the ratio $1: k$ then $k$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Straight line from Origin is $y=m x$
$4 x+2 y=3, y=m x, x_1=\frac{3}{(2 m+4)}, y_1=\frac{3 m}{(2 m+4)}$
$2 x+y+6=0, y=m x, \quad x_2=\frac{-6}{(m+2)}, y_2=\frac{-6 m}{(m+2)}$
$O=\frac{\frac{-6 m}{(m+2)}+\frac{3 m k}{2(m+2)}}{k+1} \Rightarrow O=\frac{\frac{-6}{m+2}+\frac{3 k}{2(m+2)}}{k+1}=\frac{-6+\frac{3 k}{2}}{k+1}=0 \Rightarrow \frac{3}{2} k=6, k=\frac{12}{3}=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the straight lines $a x+b y+c=0$ and $x \cos \alpha+y \sin \alpha=c$, enclose an angle $\pi / 4$ between them and meet the straight line $x \sin \alpha-y \cos \alpha=0$ in the same point then write the value of $a^2+b^2$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 3 \text { lines are concurrent } \\ & \Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc}a & b & c \\ \cos \alpha & \sin \alpha & -c \\ \sin \alpha & -\cos \alpha & 0\end{array}\right|=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{a} \cos \alpha+\mathrm{b} \sin \alpha=-1 \\ & \cos (\pi / 4)=\frac{|a \cos \alpha+b \sin \alpha|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2} \sqrt{\cos ^2 \alpha+\sin ^2 \alpha}} \\ & \Rightarrow a^2+b^2=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
